work_unit: scout-scan: add pending worktree count per project + review recommendation when all healthy
situation: All 8 projects score 0.0 (healthy), but 23 pending worktrees across 7 projects are invisible to the scan report. Human operator has no visibility into worktree backlog. When all health signals are green, scout-scan recommends 'skip or doc polish' even though there's real actionable work (review/merge/cleanup worktrees).
action: Added count_pending_worktrees() function that counts total/stale(0-ahead)/with-content worktrees per project via git rev-list --count. Integrated into scan_project output, health_priority work unit logic (recommend 'review N worktrees' when score=0 and worktrees exist), and text report (shows '待处理 worktree: 共N, 待合并=M, 空(可清理)=K' per project). Applied to both workspace copy (scripts/scout-scan.py) and AS repo copy.
outcome_evidence: python3 scripts/scout-scan.py --workspace /home/admin/workspace now shows per-project worktree counts (e.g. '待处理 worktree: 共4, 待合并=3, 空(可清理)=1') and recommends 'review 3 个待合并 worktree' instead of '无明确小工作单位——可跳过'. Verified with live scan output.
next_suggestion: Clean up the 6 stale (0-ahead) optimization worktrees across projects — they're empty starting points with no unique content, safe to remove.
